 {"id":"bd-iuvb.15","title":"Demo: Performance Challenge Mode (Degradation Tiers)","description":"Background
- Degradation tiers and budgets are critical but not demonstrated interactively.

Goals
- Provide a "stress" mode that intentionally overwhelms rendering.
- Show real-time degradation tier transitions and recovery.
- Display budget metrics and decision evidence.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Perf".
- Registry tags: "budget", "tier", "latency".

Implementation Notes
- Reuse performance HUD metrics and render budget info.
- Add deterministic stress scenarios (fixed seed, fixed workload).
- Include a "cool down" button to show recovery.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress toggle reliably triggers degradation and recovery.
- UI clearly shows current tier and decision rationale.
- Runs without stalling input handling.
- Evidence entries are recorded for each tier change.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for stress scenario generator determinism.
- Snapshot tests for each degradation tier.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, tier_from, tier_to, frame_time_ms, decision, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234397776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234379662Z","close_reason":"Completed performance challenge mode (stress harness + tier logging)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:13.446170028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103372323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103354269Z","close_reason":"Completed: palette logging, snapshots, ranking test, help legend, E2E step","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17","title":"Demo UX: Mouse-first interactivity + shortcut expansion","description":"Background
- Demo showcase already has a strong screen catalog, Screen Registry, command palette, and a Guided Tour.
- Mouse support exists but is currently shallow: tab bar click-switching is wired via the hit grid, and a handful of screens register hit regions, but most screens do not respond to mouse in an intuitive 'click what you see' way.
- Many screens have keyboard shortcuts, but lots of interaction is either hidden or passive; the demo should feel alive and exploratory.

Goals
- Make every screen in `ftui-demo-showcase` meaningfully mouse-friendly:
  - Clickable controls/widgets respond as users expect.
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the thing under the pointer (lists, panels, editors).
  - Drag interactions work where appropriate (kanban, drag-drop, timeline scrubbing, sliders).
  - Hover affordances are visible (focus/active state, subtle highlight, status hints).
- Add more keyboard shortcuts that *do things* (not just navigation):
  - Consistent global shortcuts (help, palette, perf HUD, inspector, toggle mouse).
  - Per-screen shortcuts for toggling modes, adjusting knobs, and triggering demos.
- Upgrade the Guided Tour storyboard to include the 'best stuff' and explicitly demonstrate:
  - Inline mode value (scrollback preserved; chrome stable).
  - Determinism + checksums + evidence ledgers.
  - Hit testing + hyperlinks + inspector overlays.
  - Performance HUD + budgets + degradation behavior.

Hard Constraints / Invariants (non-negotiable)
- Preserve Inline Mode scrollback as a first-class property:
  - Default behavior must not silently steal terminal scrollback unless user opts in.
  - Mouse capture policy must be explicit and documented in the demo UI and CLI.
- Keep one-writer rule / terminal lifecycle correctness intact.
- Determinism: fixed seeds + time-freeze remain deterministic across mouse/keyboard interactions.
- Performance: hit testing and hover updates must not materially regress frame time; coalesce high-frequency mouse move events.

Scope Notes
- This epic is about demo UX and interaction plumbing. It may require small changes in `ftui-core`/`ftui-widgets` (e.g., semantic mouse events or richer hit regions) when needed, but avoid large API churn.
- Prefer building shared primitives (clickable list/table/scrollbar/etc.) once, then applying to screens.

Success Criteria
- Every screen under `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/` has:
  - At least one meaningful mouse interaction (click/scroll/drag/hover) that changes state.
  - At least 3-5 screen-specific shortcuts that are discoverable in the help overlay.
- Guided Tour v2 covers the highest-signal screens and interactions.
- Test coverage exists:
  - Unit tests for mouse dispatch + focus/hover rules.
  - Deterministic integration tests per interaction category.
  - PTY/E2E validation where feasible.

Key References (starting points)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid enablement + tab click handler)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges for chrome/panes)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (mouse capture enable/disable)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (semantic click/drag abstractions)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` + `spatial_hit_index.rs` (hit grid implementation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58.741342904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","mouse","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":817,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution Notes / Dependency Shape
- Foundation tasks:
  - bd-iuvb.17.1 (mouse policy / inline scrollback) blocks bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) and bd-iuvb.17.6 (tour v2) and bd-iuvb.17.7 (tests).
  - bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) blocks bd-iuvb.17.4 (chrome mouse).
  - bd-iuvb.17.3 (widget hit semantics) and bd-iuvb.17.5 (shortcut standards) are shared prerequisites for essentially all screen work.
- Screen tasks are grouped under sub-epics by ScreenCategory:
  - Tour: bd-iuvb.17.8
  - Core: bd-iuvb.17.9
  - Visuals: bd-iuvb.17.10
  - Interaction: bd-iuvb.17.11
  - Text: bd-iuvb.17.12
  - Systems: bd-iuvb.17.13
- Guided Tour v2 (bd-iuvb.17.6) is explicitly blocked on the highest-signal interactive screens (dashboard, inline mode, determinism, time travel, hyperlinks, perf HUD, etc.) so we don’t ship a tour that skips the good stuff.

Suggested Parallelization
- Do bd-iuvb.17.1 and bd-iuvb.17.2 early, because they define the click/scroll/drag contract.
- bd-iuvb.17.3 (widgets) can proceed in parallel and will unblock many screens quickly.
- Once the dispatcher exists, screens can be upgraded independently by category.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","title":"Demo Input: mouse capture policy + inline scrollback preservation","description":"Background
- Mouse capture is currently available (see `ftui-core` terminal session enable/disable and the demo's `--no-mouse` CLI flag), but we must not compromise FrankenTUI's core value proposition: Inline Mode preserves native terminal scrollback.
- In many terminals, enabling mouse reporting steals the scroll wheel from the terminal's scrollback. That is acceptable in AltScreen mode, but it is a footgun in Inline mode unless it is explicit and opt-in.

Goal
- Define and implement a clear, explicit mouse capture policy for the demo showcase that:
  - Preserves native scrollback in Inline mode by default.
  - Still allows a 'full mouse' experience in AltScreen mode by default.
  - Provides an obvious, discoverable toggle and CLI/env config.

Design / Policy (proposed)
- Introduce a demo-level `MouseMode` concept (names are bikesheddable):
  - `Off`: no mouse capture; scroll wheel is terminal scrollback.
  - `On`: mouse capture enabled (click/scroll/drag/hover routed to UI).
- Defaults:
  - AltScreen: `On`.
  - Inline: `Off` (unless user explicitly opts in).
- UI affordances:
  - Status bar shows `Mouse: OFF (scrollback)` vs `Mouse: ON (UI scroll)`.
  - Help overlay documents toggle.
- Controls:
  - Keybinding: `M` toggles mouse mode.
  - CLI: keep `--no-mouse`; add `--mouse=on|off` (or env) if needed for determinism in tests.

Key Constraints
- No surprise scrollback theft in Inline mode.
- Must not violate one-writer rule or terminal teardown invariants.
- Deterministic: mode toggles are explicit inputs; no terminal-dependent heuristics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode demo retains native scrollback by default (wheel scrolls scrollback).
- AltScreen demo remains fully mouse interactive by default.
- Mouse toggle is visible/discoverable (status + help), and is logged in JSONL if demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for CLI/env parsing and default selection based on screen mode.
- PTY E2E smoke: Inline mode launch asserts mouse capture is disabled by default; AltScreen asserts enabled.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (Enable/Disable mouse capture)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` (mouse flags)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (event routing)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:14:38.206112249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","input","mouse","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":815,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyGorge claiming this bead. Will implement mouse capture policy with MouseMode enum, screen-mode-aware defaults, M keybinding toggle, status bar indicator, and CLI flags.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39Z"},{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"EmeraldAnchor","text":"Note: ftui-runtime still has a stale TODO/no-op for mouse capture. In crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs:2191-2195, Cmd::SetMouseCapture(enabled) currently ignores enabled; TerminalSession already has set_mouse_capture(bool) in crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs:393. Wiring execute_cmd to call self.session.set_mouse_capture(enabled)? should unblock demo mouse-mode work.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:14:38Z"}]}

- Mouse capture is currently available (see `ftui-core` terminal session enable/disable and the demo's `--no-mouse` CLI flag), but we must not compromise FrankenTUI's core value proposition: Inline Mode preserves native terminal scrollback.
- In many terminals, enabling mouse reporting steals the scroll wheel from the terminal's scrollback. That is acceptable in AltScreen mode, but it is a footgun in Inline mode unless it is explicit and opt-in.

Goal
- Define and implement a clear, explicit mouse capture policy for the demo showcase that:
  - Preserves native scrollback in Inline mode by default.
  - Still allows a 'full mouse' experience in AltScreen mode by default.
  - Provides an obvious, discoverable toggle and CLI/env config.

Design / Policy (proposed)

  - `Off`: no mouse capture; scroll wheel is terminal scrollback.
  - `On`: mouse capture enabled (click/scroll/drag/hover routed to UI).
- Defaults:
  - AltScreen: `On`.
  - Inline: `Off` (unless user explicitly opts in).
- UI affordances:
  - Status bar shows `Mouse: OFF (scrollback)` vs `Mouse: ON (UI scroll)`.
  - Help overlay documents toggle.
- Controls:
  - Keybinding: `M` toggles mouse mode.
  - CLI: keep `--no-mouse`; add `--mouse=on|off` (or env) if needed for determinism in tests.

Key Constraints
- No surprise scrollback theft in Inline mode.
- Must not violate one-writer rule or terminal teardown invariants.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode demo retains native scrollback by default (wheel scrolls scrollback).
- AltScreen demo remains fully mouse interactive by default.

Tests / Validation

Key References
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (Enable/Disable mouse capture)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` (mouse flags)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (event routing)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:58:57.256113089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","input","mouse","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":815,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyGorge claiming this bead. Will implement mouse capture policy with MouseMode enum, screen-mode-aware defaults, M keybinding toggle, status bar indicator, and CLI flags.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39Z"},{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"EmeraldAnchor","text":"Note: ftui-runtime still has a stale TODO/no-op for mouse capture. Wiring execute_cmd to call self.session.set_mouse_capture(enabled)? should unblock demo mouse-mode work.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:14:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Visuals screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Visuals-category screens.

Included Screens
- Data Viz
- Table Theme Gallery
- Mermaid Showcase
- Mermaid Mega Showcase
- Visual Effects
- Theme Studio
- Snapshot Player (Time Travel Studio)

Visuals Interaction Themes
- Mouse for pan/zoom/inspect where meaningful (diagrams, charts).
- Wheel adjusts scale or scrolls within the appropriate panel, never 'randomly'.
- Keyboard shortcuts trigger parameter sweeps, overlays, and reset-to-deterministic-state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","visuals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","title":"Data Viz: click-to-toggle series + mouse inspect + shortcuts","description":"Background
- DataViz is a dense visuals screen; it should support mouse inspection and fast toggles.

Goal
- Make charts feel interactive: click legends, hover points, wheel scroll/zoom where appropriate.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover a chart point shows tooltip (in status hint or overlay).
- Click legend toggles series visibility.
- Wheel adjusts zoom scale or scrolls within the selected chart panel (consistent rule).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `L`: toggle legend.
- `Z`: toggle zoom mode.
- `R`: reset view.

Acceptance Criteria
- Interactions are deterministic (same inputs -> same tooltip/selection).
- At least 3-5 shortcuts are available and discoverable.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/data_viz.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data-viz","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","title":"Table Theme Gallery: click-to-select preset + scroll + compare shortcuts","description":"Background
- TableThemeGallery is a perfect 'click around and compare' screen.

Goal
- Make theme selection and comparison mouse-first, and add shortcuts for rapid cycling.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights a preset tile.
- Click selects preset.
- Wheel scrolls preset list and/or adjusts animation phase if present.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `J/K`: next/prev preset.
- `Space`: pause/resume theme animation (if applicable).
- `C`: toggle comparison layout.

Acceptance Criteria
- Preset selection is obvious with hover/click.
- Snapshot includes selected preset state.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/table_theme_gallery.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","table-theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.963294661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","title":"Explainability Cockpit: mouse-first evidence navigation + shortcut toggles","description":"Background
- ExplainabilityCockpit is a flagship 'trust' screen.

Goal
- Mouse navigation through evidence panes should be natural; add shortcuts to toggle ledgers and overlays.

Mouse Interactions
- Click tabs/sections within the cockpit.
- Wheel scrolls evidence text.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `E`: toggle evidence overlay.
- `N/P`: next/prev evidence entry.

Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence navigation is deterministic and snapshot-friendly.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/explainability_cockpit.rs`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","explainability","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.180375905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.8","title":"VOI Overlay: clickable probes + mouse hover hints + shortcuts","description":"Background
- VOI overlay should expose 'why we measure this next' reasoning.

Goal
- Make probes clickable and add shortcuts to toggle VOI modes.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover shows VOI hint for region.
- Click selects a probe.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `V`: toggle VOI mode.
- `R`: reset.

Acceptance Criteria
- No nondeterministic sampling; VOI must be explicit and explainable.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/voi_overlay.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","voi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.492274579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.9","title":"Accessibility Panel: click toggles + keyboard navigation + clear a11y hints","description":"Background
- Accessibility panel toggles high contrast, reduced motion, large text.

Goal
- Make it a real control panel: click toggles, and shortcuts to quickly switch modes.

Mouse Interactions
- Click each toggle.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `H`: high contrast.
- `M`: reduced motion.
- `L`: large text.

Acceptance Criteria
- Changes propagate immediately across demo.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/accessibility_panel.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.9","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.9","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.9","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.9","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:05.786694072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","title":"Demo Input: central mouse dispatcher (hit grid + semantic events)","description":"Background
- The demo already enables hit testing (`Frame::enable_hit_testing`) and caches a hit grid for tab-bar navigation.
- Today, only the tab bar click path is routed (`handle_mouse_tab_click`). Many screens already register hit regions (panes, links, etc.) but they are not wired to input.

Goal
- Build a central, deterministic mouse dispatch layer in the demo app that routes mouse events to the right target:
  - Chrome (tabs, category tabs, status bar toggles).
  - Overlays (command palette, help overlay, tour overlay, modal stacks).
  - Active screen content.

Scope / Work Items
- Standardize the input pipeline:
  - Raw `Event::Mouse(MouseEvent)` -> (optional) semantic gestures -> dispatch.
  - Decide click activation edge: prefer `MouseUp` for buttons (prevents accidental drags) unless explicitly needed.
- Hit test on every relevant mouse event:
  - Use `(HitId, HitRegion, HitData)` from the cached hit grid.
  - Provide a consistent mapping from hit targets to actions.
- Add hover tracking:
  - Mouse move updates a `hover_hit` state (coalesced) to support hover highlight + status hints.
- Drag handling:
  - Minimal drag state machine for screens needing drag (kanban, drag-drop, sliders, timeline scrub).
  - Use existing `ftui-core` semantic event primitives if/when helpful.

Determinism / Performance Considerations
- Coalesce mouse move events aggressively (existing event coalescer already helps).
- Avoid per-frame allocations for routing; keep hit ids stable.
- Never introduce wall-clock time dependencies in gesture recognition unless time-freeze is part of determinism mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any registered hit region produces exactly one deterministic action.
- Hover state updates do not cause flicker and are coalesced.
- Drag interactions have a well-formed lifecycle and can be cancelled.
- Mouse interactions stop the guided tour consistently (like tab clicks already do).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for routing priority (palette > help > chrome > screen) and for hit mapping.
- Deterministic integration test: simulate a sequence of mouse events and assert resulting state transitions.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid cache + current mouse handling)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (click/drag semantics)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid API)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.260604738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","demo-showcase","hit-testing","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.260566256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","title":"Widgets: standard hit regions + mouse semantics for core widgets","description":"Background
- The render layer supports hit testing via a per-cell hit grid.
- Many widgets already accept `hit_id(...)` and register hit regions in frames, but mouse semantics are not uniformly defined or exploited in the demo.

Goal
- Define and implement consistent mouse semantics for the core widget primitives used throughout the demo:
  - Lists, tables, trees, scrollbars, buttons/toggles, modals.

Scope / Work Items
- Hit region conventions:
  - Row/entry hit data encodes index (stable) so click can select/activate the right item.
  - Scrollbar hit regions include track/thumb and provide proportional scroll-to behavior.
  - Buttons/toggles use `HitRegion::Button` and activate on mouse up.
- Interaction semantics:
  - Click selects.
  - Double click activates/opens (where meaningful).
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the widget under hover/focus.
  - Drag thumb scroll works on scrollbars.
- Accessibility:
  - Hover and focus states are visible with high-contrast + large-text modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- The same mouse gestures behave the same across all screens using these widgets.
- Hit IDs and hit data are stable and deterministic across frames.

Tests / Validation
- Extend `ftui-widgets` frame integration tests for hit registration and hit data correctness.
- Add snapshot tests showing hover/selected states for at least one list and one table.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid)
- `crates/ftui-widgets/*` (widgets)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:02:16.803572274Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui-widgets","hit-testing","mouse","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":818,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FuchsiaHill claiming this bead. Will implement consistent hit regions + mouse semantics for core widgets (lists, tables, trees, scrollbars, buttons/toggles, modals).","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:02:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","title":"Demo Chrome: tabs/palette/help/status overlays fully mouse interactive","description":"Background
- The demo chrome renders the tab bar, status bar, help overlay, and command palette.
- Tab bar hit regions exist and tab switching works, but most other chrome is keyboard-only.

Goal
- Make the demo's chrome and overlays fully mouse-clickable and scrollable in the obvious way.

Scope / Work Items
- Tab bar:
  - Hover highlight, click-to-switch (already exists), and optional scroll-wheel-to-cycle.
- Category tabs (if rendered):
  - Click category pill to jump.
- Command palette:
  - Hover highlight; click selects; double click executes; wheel scrolls list.
- Help overlay:
  - Clickable close button; wheel scroll if content overflows.
- Status bar:
  - Clickable affordances for the most common toggles (help, palette, a11y, perf HUD, debug) without ambiguity.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can navigate the entire demo UI with mouse alone (except text input entry).
- Mouse interactions are logged when demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for hit-id mapping for chrome elements.
- Snapshot tests for hover states + palette selection.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs`
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.544868719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chrome","command-palette","demo-showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.544822323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","title":"Demo Shortcuts: global key standards + per-screen interactive keybindings","description":"Background
- The demo already supports a help overlay and per-screen keybinding lists.
- Many screens still feel passive; adding meaningful shortcuts is the easiest way to make the demo feel like a 'lab' rather than a gallery.

Goal
- Establish a consistent global shortcut vocabulary and expand per-screen shortcuts so each screen is discoverably interactive.

Global Shortcut Standards (proposed)
- `?` toggle help overlay.
- `Ctrl+K` command palette.
- `Ctrl+P` performance HUD.
- `Ctrl+I` inspector / hit regions overlay (if present).
- `M` toggle mouse mode (see mouse policy task).
- `Esc` closes the topmost overlay (palette/help/modal/tour).

Per-Screen Requirements
- Each screen must have at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts that change state or demonstrate a capability.
- Every shortcut must appear in help overlay under the active screen.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shortcut set is coherent across screens (same key means same kind of action).
- No conflicts with text input screens (respect focus).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for help overlay including global bindings + screen bindings.
- Snapshot tests for help overlay at 80x24 and 120x40 for at least 3 representative screens.","notes":"Claiming: will consolidate global shortcut vocabulary + ensure help overlay stays in sync; start by auditing current global keys and adding regression tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentLantern","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:00:09.977116912Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","help","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","title":"Guided Tour v2: add missing best-of moments + mouse/shortcut narration","description":"Background
- Guided tour mode exists and is deterministic, but the storyboard is currently not showcasing some of the most compelling screens and interactions.

Goal
- Expand and refine the guided tour to highlight the highest-signal demo features, with explicit narration of mouse/keyboard interactions.

Scope / Work Items
- Storyboard updates:
  - Ensure tour covers: Inline mode story, determinism lab, time-travel/snapshots, hit testing/hyperlinks, performance HUD/budgeting, and at least one 'big visual' (VFX/Quake).
  - Include at least 2 'interactive moments' where the tour explicitly demonstrates a click/drag/scroll or keypress sequence.
- Overlay copy updates:
  - Callouts should point out the exact interaction ('Click the tab', 'Scroll here', 'Press Ctrl+K').
  - Keep copy tight and high-signal.

Constraints
- Tour remains deterministic under fixed seed and time-freeze.
- Tour must not depend on real wall-clock delays.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tour completes in ~2-3 minutes and feels like a cinematic product tour.
- Tour demonstrates mouse and shortcuts explicitly.
- Tour does not break inline scrollback story.

Tests / Validation
- Update existing tour E2E harness to cover new steps and assert stable step ordering/log output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.512011698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.439885192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.203082627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.511911070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.979851049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.383701981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.677897754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.893529658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.286884579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.592109121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.144673588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.841142653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.477825496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.778447577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.073821838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
